title: Beta Feedback Form with Linear Integration

tasks.title: Create packages/feedback/ package scaffold
tasks.description:
Initialize the `packages/feedback/` package with `package.json` (name: `@repo/feedback`), `tsconfig.json` extending `@repo/typescript-config`, and `src/index.ts` entry point. Add dependencies: `zod`, `react`, `ua-parser-js`. Add devDependencies: `@repo/typescript-config`, `vitest`. Register the package in the root `pnpm-workspace.yaml` if needed. Verify the package builds and is importable from other packages.

**Test requirements:**
- Unit test: verify package exports are accessible (`packages/feedback/test/index.test.ts`)
- Verify tsconfig compiles without errors

subtasks.title: Create package.json with name, dependencies, scripts
Verify package compiles and is importable

tasks.title: Define Zod schemas for feedback form payload
tasks.description:
Create `packages/feedback/src/schemas/feedback.schema.ts` with the `feedbackFormSchema` Zod schema as defined in spec section 3.6. Include: step 1 fields (type enum, title, description), step 2 optional fields (severity enum, stepsToReproduce, expectedResult, actualResult, attachments as File array), user info (reporterEmail, reporterName), and environment object (currentUrl, browser, os, viewport, timestamp, appSource enum, deployVersion, userId, consoleErrors array, errorInfo object). Export inferred TypeScript types (`FeedbackFormData`, `FeedbackEnvironment`). Also create a server-side variant schema that accepts attachments as `Buffer` instead of `File` for API validation.

**Test requirements:**
- Unit tests in `packages/feedback/test/schemas/feedback.schema.test.ts`
- Test valid payloads pass validation
- Test each required field missing triggers error
- Test enum values reject invalid values
- Test min/max length constraints on title (5-200) and description (10-5000)
- Test optional fields can be omitted
- Test email validation
- Test environment sub-object validation

subtasks.title: Define server-side variant for Buffer attachments

tasks.title: Create feedback config file with configurable settings
tasks.description:
Create `packages/feedback/src/config/feedback.config.ts` with all configurable values as defined in spec sections 3.1-3.4. Include: `REPORT_TYPES` array (6 types with id, label in Spanish, linearLabelId placeholders), `SEVERITY_LEVELS` array (4 levels with id, label, description, linearPriority), `LINEAR_CONFIG` object (teamId, projectId, defaultStateId, labels for source and environment), and `FEEDBACK_CONFIG` master object (linear, reportTypes, severityLevels, fallbackEmail, rateLimit: 30, linearMaxRetries: 3, maxFileSize: 10MB, maxAttachments: 5, keyboardShortcut, enabled). All values use `as const` for type safety.

**Test requirements:**
- Unit tests in `packages/feedback/test/config/feedback.config.test.ts`
- Verify all config values are readonly
- Verify REPORT_TYPES has 6 entries with correct structure
- Verify SEVERITY_LEVELS maps to correct linearPriority values (1-4)
- Verify FEEDBACK_CONFIG merges all sub-configs

tasks.title: Create hardcoded Spanish strings constants file
tasks.description:
Create `packages/feedback/src/config/strings.ts` with the `FEEDBACK_STRINGS` constant as defined in spec section 9. Contains all user-facing text in Spanish organized by section: fab, form, fields, buttons, techDetails, success, errorBoundary, validation, rateLimit. All values as `as const`.

**Test requirements:**
- Unit test in `packages/feedback/test/config/strings.test.ts`
- Verify all keys exist and are non-empty strings
- Verify no English text accidentally included

subtasks.title: Define FEEDBACK_STRINGS constant with all sections
Write unit tests verifying all keys

tasks.title: Add feedback environment variables to @repo/config
tasks.description:
Add the following environment variables to `@repo/config`: `HOSPEDA_LINEAR_API_KEY` (string, required in production), `HOSPEDA_FEEDBACK_FALLBACK_EMAIL` (string, default 'feedback@hospeda.com'), `HOSPEDA_FEEDBACK_ENABLED` (boolean, default true). Follow existing patterns in the config package for env var registration and validation.

**Test requirements:**
- Verify env vars are properly typed and validated
- Verify defaults work when env vars are not set

subtasks.title: Add Zod validation for new env vars

tasks.title: Export feedback schemas to @repo/schemas
tasks.description:
Re-export the feedback Zod schemas and inferred types from `@repo/schemas` so they are available to other packages. Add a re-export barrel in `packages/schemas/src/feedback/` or append to existing exports. Ensure `FeedbackFormData` and `feedbackFormSchema` are accessible as `@repo/schemas/feedback`.

**Test requirements:**
- Verify import from `@repo/schemas` works
- Verify types are properly re-exported

tasks.title: Add attachments field to SendEmailInput interface
tasks.description:
In `packages/notifications/src/transports/email/` (or wherever `SendEmailInput` is defined), add an optional `attachments` field: `attachments?: Array<{ filename: string; content: Buffer | string; contentType?: string; }>`. This matches the Resend SDK's native attachment format.

**Test requirements:**
- Unit test: verify the interface accepts attachments
- Verify existing code compiles without changes (backward compatible)

tasks.title: Pass attachments through ResendEmailTransport.send()
tasks.description:
In `packages/notifications/src/transports/email/resend-transport.ts`, update the `send()` method to include `attachments` when calling `this.resend.emails.send()`. Resend SDK already supports attachments natively, so this is just passing the field through. If attachments is undefined, no change to behavior.

**Test requirements:**
- Unit test in `packages/notifications/test/transports/resend-transport.test.ts`
- Test that attachments are passed to Resend SDK when provided
- Test that omitting attachments works as before (backward compatible)
- Mock the Resend SDK to verify the payload

subtasks.title: Write unit tests with Resend mock

tasks.title: Add skipDb and skipLogging options to NotificationService.send()
tasks.description:
In `packages/notifications/src/` (NotificationService class), add a `SendNotificationOptions` interface with `skipDb?: boolean` and `skipLogging?: boolean`. Update the `send()` method signature to accept this options object. When `skipDb` is true, skip writing to `billing_notification_log` table. When `skipLogging` is true, skip structured logging via `@repo/logger`. Both default to false for backward compatibility.

**Test requirements:**
- Unit tests in `packages/notifications/test/notification-service.test.ts`
- Test normal flow (no options) still logs and writes to DB
- Test skipDb=true skips DB write
- Test skipLogging=true skips logger calls
- Test both combined

subtasks.title: Update send() method to accept options
Add skipDb conditional around DB write
Add skipLogging conditional around logger calls
Write unit tests for all combinations

tasks.title: Add FEEDBACK_REPORT to NotificationType enum and template mapping
tasks.description:
In `packages/notifications/`, add `FEEDBACK_REPORT` to the `NotificationType` enum. Add a corresponding entry in `NOTIFICATION_CATEGORY_MAP`. Add a case in `selectTemplate()` that maps to the FeedbackReportEmail template (created in T-011). Add a subject pattern in `subject-builder.ts` using format `[{type}] {title}`.

**Test requirements:**
- Verify enum includes new value
- Verify selectTemplate returns correct template
- Verify subject builder formats correctly

tasks.title: Create FeedbackReportEmail React Email template
tasks.description:
Create `packages/notifications/src/templates/feedback/FeedbackReportEmail.tsx`. This React Email component renders a full feedback report as an HTML email. Uses existing shared components (`EmailLayout`, `EmailHeading`, `InfoRow`). Sections: Report header (type badge, title), Reporter info (name, email), Description, Severity (if provided), Steps to reproduce (if provided), Expected/Actual result (if provided), Environment details table (URL, browser, OS, viewport, version, console errors), Timestamps. All text in Spanish.

**Test requirements:**
- Snapshot test of rendered output
- Test with minimal data (step 1 only)
- Test with full data (step 1 + step 2 + all environment fields)
- Verify all fields are rendered when provided

subtasks.title: Add report header section with type badge
Add reporter info, description, severity sections
Write snapshot and data variation tests

tasks.title: Write integration tests for notifications extensions
tasks.description:
Write comprehensive integration tests verifying all `@repo/notifications` extensions work together: sending an email with attachments, skipDb, skipLogging, and the FEEDBACK_REPORT template. Use mocked Resend client and mocked DB.

**Test requirements:**
- Integration test in `packages/notifications/test/feedback-integration.test.ts`
- Test full flow: create FEEDBACK_REPORT notification with attachments, skipDb, skipLogging
- Verify Resend receives correct payload including attachments
- Verify DB is NOT written to when skipDb=true
- Verify logger is NOT called when skipLogging=true
- Verify template renders correctly in the flow

subtasks.title: Set up mocks for Resend, DB, logger
Test full FEEDBACK_REPORT flow with all options

tasks.title: Create POST /api/v1/public/feedback endpoint with multipart validation
tasks.description:
Create the feedback API route in `apps/api/src/routes/public/feedback.ts`. The endpoint accepts `multipart/form-data` with form fields + file attachments. Validate the payload with the server-side feedback schema (from T-002). Validate attachments: max 5 files, max 10MB each, allowed types: image/png, image/jpeg, image/webp, image/gif. Add a honeypot hidden field for spam prevention. Return structured responses as defined in spec section 6.

**Test requirements:**
- Unit tests in `apps/api/test/routes/public/feedback.test.ts`
- Test valid payload returns 200
- Test missing required fields returns 400 with VALIDATION_ERROR
- Test invalid file type rejected
- Test file too large rejected
- Test too many files rejected
- Test honeypot field filled triggers silent rejection

subtasks.title: Create route file with multipart handling
Add Zod validation for form fields

tasks.title: Implement Linear API client for issue creation with file uploads
tasks.description:
Create `apps/api/src/lib/linear-client.ts` (or `packages/feedback/src/lib/linear-client.ts`). Implement: (1) `uploadFileToLinear({linearClient, fileBuffer, filename, contentType, size})` - calls `linearClient.fileUpload()` to get pre-signed URL, then PUTs the binary with correct headers (Content-Type is mandatory, 403 without it), returns `assetUrl`. (2) `createLinearIssue({linearClient, formData, assetUrls})` - creates issue with `issueCreate` mutation, title format `[{type}] {title}`, markdown description with all fields and embedded images as `![filename](assetUrl)`, labels from config, priority mapped from severity (default Medium/3), project and state from config. Uses `@linear/sdk`.

**Test requirements:**
- Unit tests in `test/lib/linear-client.test.ts`
- Mock `@linear/sdk` LinearClient
- Test fileUpload flow: verify correct params, verify PUT call with headers
- Test issueCreate: verify title format, description markdown, labels, priority mapping
- Test severity-to-priority mapping (critical=1, high=2, medium=3, low=4, undefined=3)
- Test assetUrls are embedded in description markdown
- Test error thrown when fileUpload fails
- Test error thrown when PUT fails (e.g., 403)

subtasks.title: Write comprehensive tests with SDK mock

tasks.title: Implement retry logic with exponential backoff
tasks.description:
Create a `withRetry` utility function in the feedback package or API. Accepts: an async function, max retries (from `FEEDBACK_CONFIG.linearMaxRetries`, default 3), and backoff delays (1s, 2s, 4s). On failure, retries with exponential backoff. On final failure, calls the email fallback via `@repo/notifications` with `skipDb: true` and `skipLogging: true`, using the `FeedbackReportEmail` template and including attachments as base64. Integrates into the feedback endpoint flow.

**Test requirements:**
- Unit tests in `test/lib/retry.test.ts`
- Test succeeds on first try: no retries
- Test succeeds on 2nd try after 1 failure
- Test succeeds on 3rd try after 2 failures
- Test all retries fail: verify email fallback is called
- Test email fallback receives correct data (report + attachments)
- Test backoff timing (verify delays between retries)
- Mock timers for fast tests

subtasks.title: Create withRetry utility with configurable retries and backoff
Implement email fallback call on final failure

tasks.title: Add rate limiting middleware for feedback endpoint
tasks.description:
Add IP-based rate limiting to the `POST /api/v1/public/feedback` endpoint. Use configurable limit from `FEEDBACK_CONFIG.rateLimit` (default 30 requests per IP per hour). Return 429 with a JSON error body including the rate limit message from `FEEDBACK_STRINGS`. Use an in-memory store (Map with TTL) for simplicity during beta. Follow existing rate limiting patterns in the API if any exist.

**Test requirements:**
- Unit tests in `apps/api/test/middleware/rate-limit.test.ts`
- Test requests under limit pass through
- Test request at limit returns 429
- Test different IPs have independent limits
- Test limit resets after TTL window

tasks.title: Write full endpoint integration tests for feedback API
tasks.description:
Comprehensive integration tests for the complete `POST /api/v1/public/feedback` endpoint flow. Mock Linear SDK and notifications. Test the full pipeline: validation -> file upload -> issue creation -> response. Test error paths: Linear failure with retries -> email fallback.

**Test requirements:**
- Integration tests in `apps/api/test/routes/public/feedback.integration.test.ts`
- Test happy path: valid payload -> Linear issue created -> 200 with issueId
- Test happy path with attachments: files uploaded to Linear -> embedded in description
- Test Linear failure: all retries fail -> email fallback sent -> 200 with fallback message
- Test rate limiting: 31st request in an hour -> 429
- Test validation errors: various invalid payloads -> 400
- Test file validation: oversized file, wrong type -> 400
- Test honeypot triggered -> silent 200 (no issue created)

subtasks.title: Set up test fixtures and mocks
Write Linear failure + email fallback tests

tasks.title: Build environment data collector utility
tasks.description:
Create `packages/feedback/src/lib/collector.ts`. Pure function `collectEnvironmentData({appSource, deployVersion?, userId?, userEmail?, userName?})` that returns the environment object: reads `window.location.href`, parses `navigator.userAgent` with `ua-parser-js` for browser name+version and OS name+version, reads `window.innerWidth`x`window.innerHeight`, generates ISO timestamp, and merges provided auth data. All values are strings. Handles SSR gracefully (returns empty strings when window is not available).

**Test requirements:**
- Unit tests in `packages/feedback/test/lib/collector.test.ts`
- Mock window/navigator objects
- Test browser detection from various user agents (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, mobile)
- Test OS detection (Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, Android)
- Test viewport format
- Test SSR fallback (no window)

tasks.title: Build useAutoCollect React hook
tasks.description:
Create `packages/feedback/src/hooks/useAutoCollect.ts`. React hook that calls `collectEnvironmentData()` on mount and returns the environment data object. Accepts `appSource` and optional auth context (userId, userEmail, userName) as params. Also reads the console error buffer from `useConsoleCapture` (T-020). Returns a mutable state so the user can edit values before submission.

**Test requirements:**
- Unit tests in `packages/feedback/test/hooks/useAutoCollect.test.ts`
- Test hook returns environment data on mount
- Test auth data is included when provided
- Test values are editable (state updates)
- Use React Testing Library renderHook

tasks.title: Build useConsoleCapture hook for console.error interception
tasks.description:
Create `packages/feedback/src/hooks/useConsoleCapture.ts`. On mount, wraps `console.error` with an interceptor that stores calls in a circular buffer (max 10 entries). Each entry: `{timestamp: ISO string, message: string}` where message is the serialized arguments truncated to 500 chars. The interceptor is passive (still calls original `console.error`). Returns function `getConsoleErrors()` to retrieve the buffer. Cleanup on unmount restores original `console.error`.

**Test requirements:**
- Unit tests in `packages/feedback/test/hooks/useConsoleCapture.test.ts`
- Test console.error calls are captured
- Test buffer is circular (11th call evicts 1st)
- Test message truncation at 500 chars
- Test original console.error is still called
- Test cleanup restores original on unmount

subtasks.title: Implement circular buffer with max 10 entries

tasks.title: Build useKeyboardShortcut hook
tasks.description:
Create `packages/feedback/src/hooks/useKeyboardShortcut.ts`. React hook that listens for `Ctrl+Shift+F` (or `Cmd+Shift+F` on macOS) keyboard events. Accepts a callback function and calls it when the shortcut is pressed. Detects macOS via `navigator.platform` or `navigator.userAgentData`. Uses `useEffect` with `keydown` event listener. Cleans up on unmount. Does not conflict with browser defaults (Ctrl+Shift+F is not a standard browser shortcut).

**Test requirements:**
- Unit tests in `packages/feedback/test/hooks/useKeyboardShortcut.test.ts`
- Test callback is called on Ctrl+Shift+F
- Test callback is called on Cmd+Shift+F (macOS)
- Test callback is NOT called on just Ctrl+F
- Test cleanup removes listener on unmount

subtasks.title: Write tests with simulated keyboard events

tasks.title: Build StepBasic component (form step 1)
tasks.description:
Create `packages/feedback/src/components/steps/StepBasic.tsx`. React component for step 1 of the feedback form. Uses shadcn components (Select, Input, Textarea). Fields: report type (Select from REPORT_TYPES), title (Input, min 5 max 200), description (Textarea, min 10 max 5000), email (Input, shown if not authenticated), name (Input, shown if not authenticated). Shows validation errors from FEEDBACK_STRINGS. Pre-fills email/name from auth context if available.

**Test requirements:**
- Component tests in `packages/feedback/test/components/steps/StepBasic.test.tsx`
- Test all 6 report types render in select
- Test validation errors show on submit with empty fields
- Test email/name fields hidden when authenticated
- Test email/name fields shown and required when not authenticated
- Test pre-fill from auth context

subtasks.title: Build report type Select with REPORT_TYPES

tasks.title: Build StepDetails component (form step 2)
tasks.description:
Create `packages/feedback/src/components/steps/StepDetails.tsx`. React component for step 2 (optional). Fields: severity (Select from SEVERITY_LEVELS with descriptions), steps to reproduce (Textarea), expected result (Textarea), actual result (Textarea), file upload (drag-and-drop or click, max 5 files, max 10MB each, image types only, preview thumbnails), and collapsible 'Technical details' section showing all auto-collected environment data as editable inputs. Uses FEEDBACK_STRINGS for all labels.

**Test requirements:**
- Component tests in `packages/feedback/test/components/steps/StepDetails.test.tsx`
- Test severity select renders all 4 levels with descriptions
- Test all text fields accept input
- Test file upload accepts valid images
- Test file upload rejects invalid types
- Test file upload rejects oversized files
- Test technical details section is collapsible
- Test environment data fields are editable

subtasks.title: Build file upload with drag-and-drop and preview

tasks.title: Build FeedbackForm component orchestrating steps and submission
tasks.description:
Create `packages/feedback/src/components/FeedbackForm.tsx`. Main form component that orchestrates StepBasic and StepDetails. Manages form state across steps. Step 1 is shown initially with 'Enviar' and 'Agregar mas detalles' buttons. Clicking 'Agregar mas detalles' shows step 2 with a 'Volver' button. 'Enviar' is available on both steps. On submit, combines step 1 + step 2 + auto-collected data into the full payload. Uses `useFeedbackSubmit` hook for API call. Shows success state (issue ID, thank you message) or error state. 'Enviar otro' resets the form.

**Test requirements:**
- Component tests in `packages/feedback/test/components/FeedbackForm.test.tsx`
- Test step 1 renders by default
- Test clicking 'Agregar mas detalles' shows step 2
- Test 'Volver' returns to step 1
- Test submit from step 1 sends minimal payload
- Test submit from step 2 sends full payload
- Test success state shows issue ID
- Test 'Enviar otro' resets form
- Test error state on API failure

tasks.title: Build FeedbackModal component (desktop modal / mobile drawer)
tasks.description:
Create `packages/feedback/src/components/FeedbackModal.tsx`. Wrapper component that renders FeedbackForm inside a shadcn Dialog (desktop, max-width 640px) or Drawer (mobile, slides up from bottom). Detects viewport with a media query (768px breakpoint). Backdrop overlay with click-to-close. Close button (X) in top-right. Escape key closes. Focus trap inside modal. Accepts `isOpen` and `onClose` props. Accepts `prefillData` prop for error boundary pre-fill.

**Test requirements:**
- Component tests in `packages/feedback/test/components/FeedbackModal.test.tsx`
- Test renders Dialog on desktop viewport
- Test renders Drawer on mobile viewport
- Test close button closes modal
- Test Escape key closes modal
- Test backdrop click closes modal
- Test prefillData is passed to FeedbackForm

subtasks.title: Add focus trap and Escape key handling
Write component tests for both viewports

tasks.title: Build FeedbackFAB component with minimize and pulse
tasks.description:
Create `packages/feedback/src/components/FeedbackFAB.tsx`. Floating action button component. Position: fixed bottom-right, 24px from edges. Size: 48px (mobile), 56px (desktop). Uses bug/message icon from `@repo/icons`. Hardcoded light mode styles. Z-index above content, below modals. Tooltip 'Reportar un problema (Ctrl+Shift+F)'. Subtle pulse animation every 30s (respects `prefers-reduced-motion`). Minimize button: shrinks to 24px dot, hover expands temporarily, state in `localStorage('feedback-fab-minimized')`. Uses `useKeyboardShortcut` hook. Clicking opens FeedbackModal. Renders FeedbackModal internally.

**Test requirements:**
- Component tests in `packages/feedback/test/components/FeedbackFAB.test.tsx`
- Test FAB renders with correct size
- Test click opens modal
- Test keyboard shortcut opens modal
- Test minimize shrinks to dot
- Test minimized state persists in localStorage
- Test hover on minimized FAB expands
- Test tooltip text

subtasks.title: Build FAB button with positioning and icon

tasks.title: Build useFeedbackSubmit hook for multipart API submission
tasks.description:
Create `packages/feedback/src/hooks/useFeedbackSubmit.ts`. React hook that handles submitting the feedback form to the API. Builds a `FormData` object from the form payload (serializes JSON fields, appends File objects). Posts to the configured API URL (`/api/v1/public/feedback`). Manages loading, success, and error states. On success, extracts linearIssueId and linearIssueUrl from response. On error, extracts error details. Accepts the API base URL as a config prop.

**Test requirements:**
- Unit tests in `packages/feedback/test/hooks/useFeedbackSubmit.test.ts`
- Mock fetch
- Test successful submission returns issue data
- Test FormData is built correctly with files
- Test loading state during submission
- Test error state on API error
- Test rate limit error (429) shows correct message

subtasks.title: Implement fetch call with loading state

tasks.title: Build query param serialization/deserialization for standalone
tasks.description:
Create `packages/feedback/src/lib/query-params.ts`. Two functions: (1) `serializeFeedbackParams({type, title, description, url, error, source, errorStack})` - encodes form pre-fill data as URL query params, truncates long values (stack trace max 500 chars), URL-encodes all values. (2) `parseFeedbackParams(searchParams: URLSearchParams)` - reads and sanitizes query params, returns partial form data for pre-filling. Sanitizes against XSS (strips HTML tags, script content).

**Test requirements:**
- Unit tests in `packages/feedback/test/lib/query-params.test.ts`
- Test round-trip: serialize -> parse returns same data
- Test long stack trace is truncated
- Test URL encoding/decoding
- Test XSS sanitization (strips <script> tags)
- Test missing params return undefined (not errors)
- Test empty string params are treated as undefined

subtasks.title: Write round-trip and edge case tests

tasks.title: Build FeedbackErrorBoundary component
tasks.description:
Create `packages/feedback/src/components/FeedbackErrorBoundary.tsx`. React error boundary (class component with `componentDidCatch`). On error: shows friendly UI from FEEDBACK_STRINGS (title, message, 'Reportar este error' button, 'Recargar' button). Clicking 'Reportar este error' tries to open FeedbackModal inline (pre-filled with error message, stack trace, current URL, type='bug-js'). If modal fails to render (widget JS broken), falls back to opening a new tab with the standalone form URL + serialized error data in query params (using T-028). Reload button calls `window.location.reload()`.

**Test requirements:**
- Component tests in `packages/feedback/test/components/FeedbackErrorBoundary.test.tsx`
- Test error boundary catches child errors
- Test friendly error UI renders with correct text
- Test 'Reportar este error' opens modal with pre-filled data
- Test 'Recargar' calls window.location.reload
- Test pre-fill data includes error message and stack
- Test fallback to new tab when modal render fails

subtasks.title: Implement inline modal opening with pre-fill
Implement new tab fallback with query params

tasks.title: Update packages/feedback/src/index.ts with all public exports
tasks.description:
Update the barrel export file `packages/feedback/src/index.ts` to export all public API: components (FeedbackFAB, FeedbackModal, FeedbackForm, FeedbackErrorBoundary), hooks (useAutoCollect, useConsoleCapture, useKeyboardShortcut, useFeedbackSubmit), config (FEEDBACK_CONFIG, FEEDBACK_STRINGS, REPORT_TYPES, SEVERITY_LEVELS), schemas (feedbackFormSchema, FeedbackFormData), and utilities (serializeFeedbackParams, parseFeedbackParams). All named exports.

**Test requirements:**
- Verify all exports are accessible from `@repo/feedback`

subtasks.title: Add config, schema, and utility exports

tasks.title: Write component integration tests for feedback form flow

End-to-end component tests verifying the full form flow within the package, using Testing Library. Mock the API endpoint.

**Test requirements:**

- Test: open FAB -> modal opens -> fill step 1 -> submit -> success shown
- Test: open FAB -> fill step 1 -> go to step 2 -> fill severity -> submit -> success
- Test: open FAB -> fill step 1 -> go to step 2 -> go back -> step 1 data preserved

tasks.title: Integrate FeedbackFAB in apps/web BaseLayout as React island
tasks.description:
Add `FeedbackFAB` from `@repo/feedback` to `apps/web-old/src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro` (or the new `apps/web` BaseLayout if migrated). Render as a React island with `client:idle` directive. Pass `appSource='web'` and `apiUrl` props. Pass auth context (userId, userEmail, userName) from the Astro page's server-side auth check if available. The FAB should appear on every page.

- Verify it uses `client:idle` (does not block page load)
- Verify auth context is passed when user is logged in

tasks.title: Integrate FeedbackErrorBoundary in apps/web React islands
tasks.description:
Wrap key React islands in `apps/web` with `FeedbackErrorBoundary` from `@repo/feedback`. Identify the main React islands that could crash (search components, filter sidebars, carousels, etc.) and wrap them. Pass `standaloneUrl` prop pointing to `/es/feedback`. The error boundary should catch errors and show the report UI without breaking the rest of the Astro page.

**Test requirements:**

- Verify the rest of the page still renders when one island crashes

tasks.title: Update apps/web 500.astro with link to standalone feedback form
tasks.description:
Update `apps/web-old/src/pages/500.astro` (or new `apps/web` equivalent). Add a 'Reportar este error' link/button that navigates to `/es/feedback?type=bug-js&source=web&error=Error+interno+del+servidor`. Use hardcoded Spanish text. The link should work even if all JS fails (plain HTML link). Style consistent with error page.

tasks.title: Create standalone feedback page at /[lang]/feedback.astro
tasks.description:
Create `apps/web-old/src/pages/[lang]/feedback.astro` (or new `apps/web` equivalent). Minimal layout: no header, no footer, no sidebar. Just a centered container with the `FeedbackForm` React component rendered with `client:load`. Reads query params and passes them as `prefillData` prop to FeedbackForm (using `parseFeedbackParams` from `@repo/feedback`). Email and name always required (no auth context on standalone). Pass `appSource='standalone'`. Include a plain HTML `mailto:` fallback link at the bottom in case even this page fails. Minimal CSS (Tailwind utilities only).

**Test requirements:**
- Verify page renders at /es/feedback
- Verify query params are parsed and passed to form
- Verify email and name are required
- Verify mailto fallback link exists
- Verify no heavy layout dependencies (no header/footer)

tasks.title: Integrate FeedbackErrorBoundary in apps/admin router
tasks.description:
Wrap the admin app's router outlet (TanStack Start) with `FeedbackErrorBoundary` from `@repo/feedback`. Pass `standaloneUrl` pointing to the web app's standalone form URL. This catches any unhandled errors in admin pages and shows the report UI.

**Test requirements:**
- Verify error boundary wraps the router
- Verify caught errors show the feedback report UI
- Verify standalone URL points to web app's /feedback page

tasks.title: Responsive testing: modal on desktop, drawer on mobile
tasks.description:
Manual + automated responsive testing. Verify FeedbackModal renders as Dialog on desktop (>= 768px) and Drawer on mobile (< 768px). Test at breakpoints: 320px, 768px, 1024px, 1440px. Verify form fields are usable on small screens. Verify file upload works on mobile. Verify keyboard shortcut tooltip shows correct key for platform.

**Test requirements:**
- E2E tests with viewport resizing in `packages/feedback/test/e2e/responsive.test.ts`
- Test Dialog renders at 1024px width
- Test Drawer renders at 375px width
- Test form is scrollable on small screens
- Test all inputs are tappable on mobile

tasks.title: Accessibility audit: keyboard nav, focus management, screen reader
tasks.description:
Audit and fix accessibility issues. Verify: focus is trapped inside modal when open, focus returns to FAB when modal closes, all form fields have labels, select dropdowns are keyboard navigable, file upload is keyboard accessible, error messages are associated with fields via aria-describedby, screen reader announces modal open/close, error boundary report button is focusable, color contrast meets WCAG AA in hardcoded light mode styles.

**Test requirements:**
- Accessibility tests in `packages/feedback/test/a11y/accessibility.test.tsx`
- Test focus trap in modal
- Test focus return on close
- Test all fields have accessible labels
- Test keyboard navigation through form
- Test ARIA attributes on error messages
- Run axe-core on rendered form

tasks.title: Rate limiting E2E verification

- Send request from different IP -> succeeds (independent limits)
- Wait for TTL window -> rate limit resets
